channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: The AI risk that few organizations are governing
item.description: You know how many human users have access to your financial systems. Do you know how many AI agents do?

item.title: Four Questions About The Iran War That Deserve Clear, Consistent Answers
item.description: We’re more than a week into the Iran war and yet we have no clear or consistent objectives, no theory of victory.

item.title: California Voter ID Ballot Measure Offers Roadmap For Enacting Common-Sense Policies In Blue States
item.description: Conservatives should do more to leverage initiatives and referenda to enact popular policies over the objections of Democrat politicians.

item.title: Blue-state governors move to keep heat on Noem as DHS fires back
item.description: Democratic governors vow to pursue accountability for Kristi Noem despite her departure from office, promising investigations into immigration enforce...

item.title: Terror in Mamdani’s New York
item.description: Hey, some people in New York City throw bombs, and others don’t. Diversity is our strength!

item.title: 'Serious concerns': GOP sounds alarm on taxpayer funds going to 'high risk' universities vulnerable to CCP
item.description: National security concerns raised over $67 million research initiative as institutions accused of partnerships with Chinese military-linked organizati...

item.title: Talarico the Texas Trickster
item.description: He is not, in fact, a moderate, unless ‘moderate’ is now a synonym for ‘white man.’

item.title: Jimmy Lai’s Free-Market Witness
item.description: The Hong Kong dissident’s persecution is a reminder that capitalism and civil liberties reinforce one another.

item.title: The Realist Case for the Iran War
item.description: It is not too late for the president and his team to be clear about why we did this.

item.title: AI can double output. Human biology can’t
item.description: There's an AI burnout trap, and your productivity gains are fragile.

item.title: Trump says Iran war is ‘very complete, pretty much’ sparking global stock rally
item.description: But also, "we’re going to go further."

item.title: Republican says ‘Muslims don’t belong in American society,’ draws fierce Democratic backlash
item.description: Republican Rep. Andy Ogles of Tennessee sparked a wave of criticism from Democrats after declaring that "Muslims don't belong in American society."

item.title: No Matter How The Iran War Ends, We Absolutely Cannot Take Any Refugees
item.description: Allowing zero refugees to resettle on American soil should be the absolute minimum requirement for any U.S. military action in the Middle East.

item.title: Democrats threaten to derail Senate business until Republicans hold public hearings on Iran war
item.description: At least a half-dozen Senate Democrats are threatening to force repetitive votes on war powers resolutions and use other tools of the minority party t...

item.title: AI isn’t killing jobs yet—CEOs are using layoffs to fund a $2.5 trillion arms race
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.

item.title: Anthropic’s Department of War lawsuit is even higher-stakes amid the AI boom
item.description: Anthropic suing the U.S. Department of War perhaps wasn’t a surprise, but it was nevertheless a shock.

item.title: House GOP fears primary losers could jeopardize razor-thin majority
item.description: House Republicans say colleagues who lose primaries could start missing votes — and with just two votes to spare after this week, the margin is tight.

item.title: Trump says he's 'not happy' with Iran's choice of new supreme leader
item.description: President Donald Trump said he's "not happy" with Iran's choice of new supreme leader, saying he does not believe Mojtaba Khamenei "can live in peace....

item.title: Brazil Threatens Citizens With Years In Prison For Saying Men Aren’t Women
item.description: Isadora Borges, a Brazilian veterinary student, faced trial last month for a post stating 'trans women are not women.'

item.title: New York Magazine’s Latest Anti-Child Propaganda Misses The Point Of Parenthood
item.description: Maybe, just maybe, sacrificing self to cultivate a legacy and life that goes beyond LinkedIn and the landfill is the point.

item.title: Rethinking Exit Multiples in High-Growth Company Valuations
item.description: Exit multiples in high-growth companies should reflect growth expectations and interest rates, not market medians. The post Rethinking Exit Multiples ...

item.title: Trump says other nations have Tomahawk missiles after strike hits school, kills more than 160 people
item.description: President Trump on Monday said other countries might have been responsible for firing a Tomahawk missile that hit an Iranian school on the first day o...

item.title: Senate leaders resist Trump pressure on talking filibuster, SAVE America Act
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune said Monday that he hopes President Trump will change his mind about not signing bills into law until the SAVE Ameri...

item.title: Iran war follows decades of the theocratic regime's deadly global terrorism
item.description: Before the U.S. and Israel opened a war on Iran, the Islamic republic had waged a nearly 50-year campaign of global terrorism that has killed thousand...

item.title: James Talarico’s Convenient Faith
item.description: When the Texas candidate cites his Christianity, it’s often in service of a trendy progressive position.

item.title: Trump vows that Iran war will be 'short-term'
item.description: President Trump told Republican House members Monday that the war in the Middle East will be only a "short-term excursion."

item.title: The Question That Exposes Weak Quant Models
item.description: Errant variables can quietly undermine even the most statistically impressive quant models. The post The Question That Exposes Weak Quant Models appea...

item.title: International Women’s Day: Women Leaders Put Nutrition First
item.description: Women are three times more likely than men to invest in eating well. The post International Women’s Day: Women Leaders Put Nutrition First appeared fi...

item.title: NLP and Yield Curve Prediction From Central Bank Minutes
item.description: Can algorithms that analyze central bank language help predict the next move in the yield curve? The post NLP and Yield Curve Prediction From Central ...

item.title: Three Levers That Drive VC Returns
item.description: VC performance hinges on ownership and failure reduction, not just rare breakout wins. The post Three Levers That Drive VC Returns appeared first on C...
